source: PK (2014) — full screenplay; weighted to the 'wrong number', god-sticker, 'Dancing Car' and the alien's literal-minded questioning of religion
love_type: both — massive box-office, AND cult for handing audiences a reusable rhetorical tool ('wrong number') to question religious middlemen
core_emotion: the liberating innocence of a child's 'but why?' — the relief of someone finally asking the obvious question everyone was too afraid to ask

dialogue_style.register: broken, hyper-literal Bhojpuri-inflected Hindi from an alien who learned language by touch — every idiom taken literally for comedy and critique
dialogue_style.rhythm: naive question → adult discomfort → naive follow-up that exposes the absurdity; relentless Socratic innocence
dialogue_style.stylization: heightened fable — an outsider device used to defamiliarize the ordinary so the audience sees their own customs as strange
dialogue_style.why_it's_theirs: the voice is a weapon: by making the hero literally unable to lie or assume, every social/religious convention has to justify itself out loud

signature_dialogues.construction: a mundane telephone metaphor repurposed as theology — if your prayer isn't working, you dialed the wrong god/the wrong middleman
signature_dialogues.the_setup: built through PK's running confusion about which of thousands of gods actually 'picks up'; he treats prayer like a call-centre
signature_dialogues.presentation: delivered with total deadpan sincerity by someone who genuinely doesn't understand he's being subversive — the innocence is what makes the heresy lovable instead of offensive
signature_dialogues.payoff: compresses the film's entire critique of religious exploitation into two everyday words anyone can deploy
signature_dialogues.why_quotable: portable to any situation where someone's been conned by a false authority; safe to say because it's 'just' a phone joke

signature_dialogues.line: Bhagwan ko khojna — that's religion. Bhagwan ka mil jaana — that's news.
signature_dialogues.construction: antithesis/reversal built on a parallel structure; reframes faith as a search vs a result
signature_dialogues.the_setup: lands amid PK's investigation of godmen who claim direct line to God
signature_dialogues.presentation: stated plainly, almost as a throwaway, letting the audience do the inference — the restraint makes it feel like wisdom rather than a slogan
signature_dialogues.payoff: punctures the godman's authority by redefining the terms
signature_dialogues.why_quotable: aphoristic, debate-ready ammunition against self-proclaimed prophets

the_peaks.moment: PK sticks god-stickers on his cheeks to claim divine protection, then tests them
the_peaks.why_loved: literalizes superstition into a visual gag that makes blind faith look absurd without lecturing

the_peaks.moment: the 'wrong number' framework dismantling the godman live on TV
the_peaks.why_loved: the little guy out-arguing a powerful fraud using only innocent logic — pure wish-fulfilment

wish_fulfillment: someone finally says out loud, without fear, the doubts about religion that ordinary people self-censor; and wins the argument
character_magnetism: PK's wide-eyed honesty — incapable of hypocrisy, so lovable that his radical questions feel like curiosity, not attack
audacity: a mainstream star-vehicle openly satirizing religious exploitation and godmen in a deeply religious market — and doing it as a feel-good comedy
portability: 'wrong number' became shorthand for misplaced faith / being conned by middlemen
why_cult_not_just_good: it gave a believing audience a SAFE, funny vocabulary to express religious doubt — a tool they could carry into real arguments
transferable_love_triggers: Use a naive outsider who takes everything literally to make the audience's own ordinary customs look strange — critique without preaching.
Compress your whole thesis into one mundane metaphor ('wrong number') that anyone can redeploy in real life.
Let the most subversive lines come from total innocence — sincerity disarms offense and makes heresy lovable.
Build aphorisms on antithesis/parallel structure ('searching = X, finding = Y') so they sound like discovered wisdom and travel as quotes.
Stage the climactic argument as the powerless out-reasoning the powerful in public — the format itself is the wish-fulfillment.
confidence: high — verbatim 'wrong number', god-sticker and the religion/news line confirmed in script
